summary: All three addressed fixes are correct and minimal. The required fix (T9-R1-01) is resolved: networks.test.ts now uses dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) matching every other test file in the suite. Branch threshold raised to 85% (T9-R1-03). Misleading oversizedHeader() comment corrected (T9-R1-05). Deferral rationale for T9-R1-02 and T9-R1-04 is sound — both are spec-vs-catalog discrepancies that originated in T8 and are correctly flagged for catalog-owner follow-up rather than silently patched in T9.

findings.title: T9-R1-01 RESOLVED
findings.description: networks.test.ts now imports dirname and fileURLToPath and uses `const here = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))` as the base for T3_ARTEFACT_PATH. The fix is exact and matches the pattern in x402.test.ts, errors.test.ts, settle.test.ts, and core.test.ts.

findings.title: T9-R1-03 RESOLVED
findings.description: branches threshold raised from 75 to 85, now aligned with lines/statements/functions. Current 89.15% branch coverage gives a 4.15% safety margin before CI fails.

findings.title: T9-R1-05 RESOLVED
findings.description: oversizedHeader() comment now accurately explains that 'A' is a valid base64 character but irrelevant because the guard fires on raw byte length pre-decode.

findings.title: T9-R1-02 DEFERRED — rationale accepted
findings.description: The relayer_low_balance payload shape divergence (task-9 spec §7 mentions threshold field; types.ts SecurityEventCatalog and core.ts emit only balanceUsdc) is correctly classified as a T8 catalog-owner issue. The test tracks the live types.ts catalog. Flagged for decisions.md follow-up. No action required in T9.

findings.title: T9-R1-04 DEFERRED — rationale accepted
findings.description: The nonce_replay vs nonce_replay_attempt event name divergence between tech-spec D18 and the live SecurityEventCatalog in types.ts is correctly classified as a T8 catalog-owner issue. core.ts emits 'nonce_replay'; the test tracks that. Flagged for decisions.md follow-up. No action required in T9.

notes: No new issues introduced by the round-2 diff. The three changed files (networks.test.ts, encode-header.ts, vitest.config.ts) contain only the targeted fixes — no unrelated changes. 244/244 tests pass; 89.15% branch coverage clears the new 85% threshold. Task 9 is ready to merge.
